title: tasks-mux: task management features - priorities, dependencies, search, bulk ops
issueSummary: tasks-mux supports breakpoint routing but lacks broader task-management primitives: priority, dependencies, search/filtering, bulk operations, richer lifecycle states, history/timeline, comments, metrics/SLA, notifications, escalation chains, structured forms, state validation, audit log, export, and backup.
issueCommentsSummary: #596 blocks #577 because tasks-mux wiring is less useful without these task-management primitives.
#597 should expose the features through CLI/MCP once the schema and backend contracts exist.
#596 is now a prerequisite for #634 and supports the unified routing hub direction from #630.
Prior planning PRs #655 and #729 exist; this artifact updates the open plan branch rather than duplicating implementation work.
requestedCapabilities: priority values low, medium, high, critical
bulk approve, close, and reassign operations
assigned, in-progress, blocked, and escalated states
notification providers for email, Slack, Discord, and webhook targets
escalation chains with fallback responders on timeout

reuseAuditFindings.finding: The active process-library root is /home/runner/.a5c/process-library/babysitter-repo/library. No repo-local .a5c/process-library directory exists.
reuseAuditFindings.planImpact: Implementation agents should use the active library references instead of assuming repo-local process-library files.
The process includes explicit keyword extraction and package scans in Phase 0.

reuseAuditFindings.finding: packages/tasks-mux/src/types.ts has BreakpointStatus pending/routed/claimed/answered/completed/expired/cancelled, Urgency low/medium/high, ResponderType, ExternalTrackerBackendConfig, and AgentMuxBackendConfig. It does not have priority critical, dependsOn, assigned/in-progress/blocked/escalated, history, comments, metrics, forms, audit, export, notification, or escalation chain schemas.
reuseAuditFindings.planImpact: Additive schema extension must reuse existing responder/backend seams and preserve existing breakpoint JSON compatibility.
The first implementation slice should extend backend contracts and typed unsupported-feature behavior before CLI/MCP work.

reuseAuditFindings.finding: packages/tasks-mux/src/backends/git-native.ts stores .breakpoints JSON and lists pending work by filesystem scan with malformed-file tolerance.
reuseAuditFindings.planImpact: Search/filter, bulk operations, history, audit, export, and compatibility fixtures should start with git-native while retaining existing file format support.

reuseAuditFindings.finding: Responder type, external-tracker backend, agent-mux backend, server backend, GitHub Issues backend, CLI, MCP, and tests already exist on staging.
reuseAuditFindings.planImpact: Do not recreate tracker or agent routing infrastructure; integrate through current public seams and capability tests.

reuseAuditFindings.finding: docs/agent-layer-gaps.md still lists the issue #596 task-management gaps.
reuseAuditFindings.planImpact: Final acceptance should update or cross-reference the gap inventory based on implemented/deferred capabilities.

implementationSlices: Phase 0: live issue read and reuse audit
Phase 1: additive architecture and compatibility design
Phase 2: failing acceptance, compatibility, backend capability, CLI, and MCP tests
Phase 3: core schemas, state machine validation, backend contract, typed unsupported features
Phase 4: git-native implementation and backend parity/capability reporting for server, GitHub Issues, external-tracker, and agent-mux
Phase 5: CLI, MCP, README, docs, and package surface parity
Phase 6: disabled-by-default notification/escalation/form providers plus audit, metrics, export, and backup guardrails
Phase 7: quality gates, adversarial review, and final acceptance
qualityGates: Reuse-audit findings are reviewed before implementation and new infrastructure reuses existing tasks-mux seams where available.
All issue-requested capabilities are implemented with tests or explicitly unsupported/deferred with rationale and follow-up references.
Existing breakpoint JSON, proven answers, CLI commands, MCP tools, and backend consumers remain backward compatible.
Shared backend capability tests cover git-native plus server, GitHub Issues, external-tracker, and agent-mux parity or typed unsupported-feature behavior.
Search/filter and bulk operations include pagination, sorting, partial failure, idempotency, and malformed-record handling tests.
State transition validation rejects invalid transitions and records history/audit entries for valid mutations.
CLI and MCP surfaces have parity tests, stable JSON output, and README/documentation coverage.
Notification and escalation providers are disabled by default, credential-safe, and tested only with mocks.
Metrics/SLA, export/backup, audit log, form, and comment behavior are deterministic and redact credentials.
git diff --check, tasks-mux tests/typecheck/build, npm run test:sdk, and npm run verify:metadata pass or document environment-only failures with exact output.
nonGoals: Do not wire the full agent stack for #577 in this process unless required for local tasks-mux tests.
Do not enable real external notification or escalation delivery by default.
Do not remove or break existing breakpoint routing, proven answers, current CLI/MCP commands, or backend JSON files.
Do not require Slack, Discord, email, webhook, tracker, or agent credentials for the default test suite.
Do not duplicate external-tracker or agent-mux infrastructure that already exists on staging.

planningNotes.processShape: Flat phased feature implementation with ATDD/TDD and quality convergence. The gap is known, so a hypotheses tree is unnecessary.
planningNotes.repoOverride: Use agent tasks instead of kind: shell subtasks. Keep breakpoints sparse and only for real scope or API decisions.
